title: SQL Server Cheat Sheet
description: Essential SQL Server commands and syntax for engineers.

commands.description: Select all columns from a table
commands.scenario: Select all columns from a table

commands.command: INSERT INTO table_name (column1, column2) VALUES (value1, value2);
commands.description: Insert a new row into a table
commands.scenario: Insert a new row into a table

commands.command: UPDATE table_name SET column1 = value1 WHERE condition;
commands.description: Update existing rows in a table
commands.scenario: Update existing rows in a table

commands.command: CREATE TABLE table_name (column1 datatype, column2 datatype);
ALTER TABLE table_name ADD column_name datatype;
commands.description: Add a new column to an existing table
commands.scenario: Add a new column to an existing table

commands.command: ALTER TABLE table_name ADD PRIMARY KEY (column_name);
commands.description: Create a primary key on a column
commands.scenario: Create a primary key on a column

commands.description: Create an index on a column
commands.scenario: Create an index on a column

commands.description: Drop an index from a table
commands.scenario: Drop an index from a table

commands.command: CREATE PROCEDURE procedure_name AS BEGIN SELECT * FROM table_name; END;

commands.description: Create a new SQL Server login
commands.scenario: Create a new SQL Server login

commands.command: USE database_name; GRANT CONNECT TO login_name;
commands.description: Grant a user access to a database
commands.scenario: Grant a user access to a database

commands.command: USE database_name; REVOKE CONNECT FROM login_name;
commands.description: Revoke a user's access to a database
commands.scenario: Revoke a user's access to a database
